attached_docs: KFM GitHub Repository Documentation Implementation Agent — Full Operating Prompt v3.1

validation_gates.reason: Scope is limited to the requested proof-lane README and the required generated-work receipt; no executable, fixture payload, workflow, schema, contract, policy, drift register, lifecycle, proof, release, runtime, or public behavior is changed.

Main advanced from eac8668ca9786773fcb015f9ba099e0e1dadeb35 to 2eb6ff628a4fd1514667c0155693558a58878cbc through unrelated Hydrology and Temporal documentation/receipt changes. The target and material evidence blobs were unchanged, so the task was re-pinned safely.

The existing pipelines/proofs/habitat_fauna_thin_slice path and document identity were retained. The conflicting Directory Rules surfaces and proposed lane ADR are made explicit; no new proof, receipt, schema, contract, policy, release, catalog, source, or documentation authority is created.

The README now distinguishes confirmed documentation, placeholder tests, fixture documentation, workflow holds, and ADR scaffold from the proposed runner, payloads, receipt schema, CI execution, release wiring, and public behavior.

The final README has one H1, one closed KFM Meta Block v2, balanced code fences, 31 resolved internal links, intentional two-space hard breaks only, no invalid trailing whitespace, and a final newline.

Inspected broad documentation and Habitat pull-request workflows use read-only contents permission and no persisted checkout credentials. CodeQL uses pull_request rather than pull_request_target and limits write permission to security-events. No deployment or publication path is introduced.

The revision contains no credentials, restricted payloads, exact sensitive-fauna locations, living-person or genomic data, private operational details, infrastructure vulnerability data, or redaction-reversal instructions.

Existing Habitat/Fauna ownership, source-role, evidence, sensitivity, lifecycle, trust-membrane, no-direct-publish, correction, rollback, and release-separation rules were retained while speculative implementation claims were narrowed.

GitHub returned README blob b9432391968c7f06947489ebc5113a52ef6d6855 on commit caac605586dab52b94a4a267d97536fa702998b4; the remote content exactly matched the prepared 29,017-byte README and SHA-256 c579983b08bdc2294c7e634e2b0065042cbd0e880dea8555a9ae6bb15784d687.
The branch was one commit ahead of the pinned base with only pipelines/proofs/habitat_fauna_thin_slice/README.md changed before this receipt was emitted.

The connector-first workflow did not clone or execute repository code. Repository evidence establishes no accepted Habitat × Fauna proof producer or substantive test command; pull-request checks are observed separately.
This receipt contains all schema-required fields, allowed truth/review/outcome enums, SHA-256 patterns, an ISO-8601 timestamp, and no undeclared top-level properties.
Human review remains pending and separate from generation, validation, mergeability, release, and publication.
